Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given a normative process description and an event log with 5 cases We need to identify which cases deviate from the intended process flow and explain the violations

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets first summarize the normative process The process always begins with Receive order Then we must check inventory Check inventory Based on inventory sufficiency we branch If inventory is sufficient Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Package items Ship order Close order If inventory is insufficient Receive order Check inventory Cancel order Close order

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Key constraints mentioned Authorize payment should only occur after Check inventory and before packaging Package items should only occur after authorization and before shipping Ship order should only occur after packaging and never after a cancellation Close order should be the final activity in all scenarios

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1 Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Package items Ship order Close order This matches the sufficient inventory flow exactly No violation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2 Receive order Check inventory Package items Authorize payment Ship order Close order But the normative process for sufficient inventory requires Receive Check Authorize payment Package Ship Close In Case 2 Package items occurs before Authorize payment According to the description Package items should only occur after authorization This is a violation

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The description says After confirming inventory the process should include the authorization of payment Once the payment is authorized the items should be prepared for shipment So packaging must follow authorization Therefore Case 2 violates the order constraint

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 3 Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Cancel order Ship order Close order This case includes both authorization of payment and cancellation and then shipping

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: According to the normative process if inventory is insufficient we should cancel and then close Here after checking inventory they authorized payment then canceled then shipped This is problematic

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The presence of Authorize payment suggests that inventory was sufficient But then they cancel Alternatively if inventory was insufficient why did they authorize payment

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: If the inventory check reveals that there is not enough stock the process must branch into a cancellation scenario In that scenario after checking inventory we should directly proceed to Cancel order So authorization should not occur in the insufficient inventory branch

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: After cancellation we should not have shipping The description says Once an order is canceled it would be illogical to continue with activities intended for fulfillment such as packaging or shipping So Ship order after Cancel order is a clear violation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: So multiple violations Authorization of payment in what appears to be an insufficient inventory scenario since cancellation follows Shipping after cancellation The sequence Authorize payment then Cancel order is contradictory

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 4 Receive order Check inventory Cancel order Close order This matches the insufficient inventory flow exactly No violation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 5 Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Ship order Close order But note that Package items is missing

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The normative process for sufficient inventory requires packaging before shipping The description says Only after items have been properly packaged is it appropriate to proceed with Ship order So shipping without packaging is a violation

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Therefore the cases that violate the normative process are Case 2 Case 3 and Case 5

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2 Violation of order constraint Package items occurs before Authorize payment The normative process requires payment authorization before packaging Case 3 Multiple violations Authorize payment occurs before Cancel order but if inventory was insufficient payment should not be authorized Ship order occurs after Cancel order which is explicitly prohibited Case 5 Violation Ship order occurs without Package items The normative process requires packaging before shipping
